heading: Key Takeaway
text: A mixture contains more than one substance (element and/ or compound) mixed in any proportion.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: IS MATTER AROUND US PURE 21 Mixtures can be separated into pure substances using appropriate separation techniques.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: A solution is a homogeneous mixture of two or more substances. The major component of a solution is called the solvent, and the minor, the solute.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The concentration of a solution is the amount of solute present per unit volume or per unit mass of the solution.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Materials that are insoluble in a solvent and have particles that are visible to naked eyes, form a suspension.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: A suspension is a heterogeneous mixture.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Colloids are heterogeneous mixtures in which the particle size is too small to be seen with the naked eye, but is big enough to scatter light. Colloids are useful in industry and daily life. The particles are called the dispersed phase and the medium in which they are distributed is called the dispersion medium.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Pure substances can be elements or compounds. An element is a form of matter that cannot be broken down by chemical reactions into simpler substances. A compound is a substance composed of two or more different types of elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements, whereas a mixture shows the properties of its constituting elements or compounds.
